109.reason: [This lookahead logic is flawed because it assumes that any token followed by '(' after two positions must be a function definition, but variable declarations can also be followed by tokens that eventually lead to '('. Moreover, preRead(2) may go out of bounds when fewer than 2 tokens remain.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [This lookahead logic is flawed because it assumes that any token followed by '(' after two positions must be a function definition, but variable declarations can also be followed by tokens that eventually lead to '('. Moreover, preRead(2) may go out of bounds when fewer than 2 tokens remain.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrect lookahead: checking preRead(2) assumes at least 3 tokens ahead, but Decl can be followed directly by FuncDef starting with 'int' or 'char', and the '(' belongs to FuncDef's parameter list. This causes premature termination of Decl parsing.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [Using preRead(2) is unsafe because it assumes there are at least 2 tokens ahead, which may cause IndexOutOfBoundsException when near end of token list. Also, this lookahead logic incorrectly assumes that any '(' at position+2 indicates a function definition, but variable declarations can also be followed by other tokens.]

121.reason: [This lookahead is also unsafe and logically incorrect. The presence of 'main' as the next token doesn't necessarily mean we've reached MainFuncDef yet—there could be other tokens in between. Also, preRead(1) may access beyond the token list boundary. Furthermore, FuncDef starts with FuncType (void/int/char), not directly with an identifier, so checking for MAINTK here is misplaced.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [This lookahead is also unsafe and logically incorrect. The presence of 'main' as the next token doesn't necessarily mean we've reached MainFuncDef yet—there could be other tokens in between. Also, preRead(1) may access beyond the token list boundary. Furthermore, FuncDef starts with FuncType (void/int/char), not directly with an identifier, so checking for MAINTK here is misplaced.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrect lookahead: checking preRead(1) assumes at least 2 tokens ahead, but MainFuncDef starts with 'int main', so when current token is 'int', preRead(1) is 'main'. However, this logic fails when there are no FuncDefs and CompUnit goes directly from Decl to MainFuncDef, causing FuncDef parsing to incorrectly consume the 'int' of MainFuncDef.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [Using preRead(1) is unsafe when at the last token, which may cause IndexOutOfBoundsException. Additionally, this logic fails to properly distinguish between function definitions and main function based solely on the next token being 'main', as it doesn't verify the complete 'int main()' pattern.]

338.reason: [This line unconditionally consumes a token without checking if it is actually an ASSIGN ('=') token. According to the grammar, '=' must be present in ConstDef, but if it's missing, this will skip whatever token is next (possibly causing cascading errors) instead of reporting a missing '=' error.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 1.0 , reason:  [This line unconditionally consumes a token without checking if it is actually an ASSIGN ('=') token. According to the grammar, '=' must be present in ConstDef, but if it's missing, this will skip whatever token is next (possibly causing cascading errors) instead of reporting a missing '=' error.]
